description: Remediation playbook for AC-2 Account Management

output.title: Account Management — Implementation Playbook

steps.action: Inventory all active accounts across all systems
steps.detail: Export user lists from Active Directory/SSO, cloud IAM (AWS, Azure), SaaS applications. Document account type (service, user, admin), owner, and last login date.
steps.evidence_artifact: Account inventory spreadsheet with last-login dates

steps.detail: Identify accounts with last_sign_in > 90 days. Create a Change Request, notify account owners 5 business days in advance, then disable (do not delete) to enable recovery if needed.
steps.evidence_artifact: Change request ticket + screenshot of disabled accounts

steps.action: Document account provisioning and deprovisioning procedures
steps.detail: Write an Account Lifecycle Standard Operating Procedure covering: access request form, manager approval, provisioning steps per system, change-of-role handling, and immediate termination procedure. Reference HR offboarding checklist.
steps.evidence_artifact: Approved Account Lifecycle SOP (PDF + version control record)

steps.detail: Schedule recurring calendar reminder for access review. Each review: export current user list, send to system owners for certification, document approvals in a signed spreadsheet or tool like Vanta/Drata. Retain records for 12 months minimum.
steps.evidence_artifact: Signed access review report with date and approver

common_pitfalls: Deleting accounts instead of disabling — causes data loss and breaks audit trails
Skipping service accounts in the review — they are frequently overprivileged and rarely reviewed
Not linking HR terminations to account deprovisioning — creates orphaned accounts within hours of termination
evidence_artifacts: Account provisioning request records with approvals
HR offboarding checklist with account revocation confirmation

description: Remediation playbook for SC-8 Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity

output.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity — TLS Hardening Playbook

steps.detail: Document every API endpoint, internal service-to-service call, and external integration. Classify each as public (internet-facing), internal (VPC), or third-party.
steps.evidence_artifact: Data flow diagram with encryption status per path

steps.action: Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum on all endpoints
steps.detail: Update load balancer and web server TLS policy to reject TLS 1.0 and 1.1. Use Mozilla SSL Configuration Generator (modern profile) as baseline. Test with testssl.sh or ssllabs.com.
steps.evidence_artifact: SSL Labs scan report showing A or A+ rating

common_pitfalls: Forgetting internal service mesh — many orgs enforce TLS externally but leave internal traffic unencrypted
Certificate expiry not monitored — leads to outages and audit findings simultaneously
evidence_artifacts: SSL Labs or testssl.sh scan report
